artifact_purpose: Initial Tier 1 extracted governance principles used to ground Phase 6 governance claims and to prevent generic programme-rule assumptions.

source_documents.notes: Programme Guide invitation to consult Programme Guide, General Annexes, and AGA; ethics appraisal and consortium agreement guidance.
General conditions; ethics, gender equality, and grant agreement implementation provisions.

no_prescribed_governance_body_names.meaning: The uploaded Tier 1 sources do not prescribe mandatory body names such as 'Project Management Board' or 'Project Coordination Team' for HE RIA/IA proposals.

no_prescribed_meeting_frequency.meaning: The uploaded Tier 1 sources do not prescribe quarterly or monthly meeting cadences for proposal governance bodies. Such cadences must come from Tier 3 or be marked as project-operational assumptions.

consortium_agreement_guidance.rule: If a consortium agreement is used, it should complement the grant agreement and must not contain provisions contrary to it.

grant_agreement_implementation_articles.rule: Implementation architecture may legitimately refer to grant-agreement implementation domains only when those references are tied to explicit grant-agreement-level provisions such as proper implementation, conflict of interest, confidentiality/security, ethics and values, data protection, IPR, communication/dissemination/open science, and specific rules for carrying out the action.

escalation_path_constraint.rule: Escalation wording may refer to coordinator/internal governance and to consortium-agreement/grant-agreement handling only if framed as a project governance route consistent with Tier 1 implementation provisions, not as unsourced generic Horizon practice.

usage_rules_for_phase6: Tier 1 governance rules constrain governance claims; they do not replace Tier 3 project-specific governance design.
If Tier 1 is silent on a specific design choice, the output must record an explicit assumption instead of claiming a 'standard RIA practice'.
Any mention of Grant Agreement or Consortium Agreement terms in governance text must be tied to a source_basis entry.
